title: EngagementOrder
description: A fire order from an authorized C2 node to an effector (Imperative 5). Published to cuas.engagement.order.{effectorId} on the T0 (safety/fires) QoS tier. The effector trusts the authorityToken (issued by the PDP), not the requesting node's say-so, and applies a final hardware interlock.

engagementId.description: Correlates the order with its status lifecycle.

requestId.description: Idempotency/correlation id for the originating operator request.

trackId.description: Target track (any sufficient-quality track, from any sensor).

effectorId.description: Engaging effector (any available effector; no prior pairing); one bus-subject-safe token.

authorityToken.description: Short-lived signed token from the Policy Decision Point authorizing THIS track+effector+window. Verified by the effector.

authorityTokenExpiresAt.description: Redundant, human-readable expiry that must exactly match the signed exp claim. The signed claim remains authoritative.

authorityTokenScope.description: Inspectable duplicate of safety-relevant signed claims. The effector verifies exact equality with the authority token.

orderSequence.description: First monotonic lifecycle sequence for this engagement. Effector status events continue from this value.

engagementType.description: Requested effect; must be compatible with the effector.

humanConfirmed.description: Signed fact that the current operator explicitly confirmed this order. Required when requireHumanConfirmation is true.

trackSnapshotTimeObserved.description: Observation time of the track at order; effector rejects if the live track is older than tolerance (anti-stale).
